final_text:
## Schema Identification

**Match: `BiospecimenTemplate`** (NF-OSI nf-metadata-dictionary)

- **File:** `./fixtures/zip/nf-osi_nf-metadata-dictionary/BiospecimenTemplate.json`
- **Identifier (`$id`):** `https://repo-prod.prod.sagebase.org/repo/v1/schema/type/registered/org.synapse.nf-biospecimentemplate`
- **Title:** `BiospecimenTemplate`

### Why this is the unique match
I searched both schema collections for files containing all four sample fields (`individualID`, `specimenID`, `aliquotID`, `bodySite`). Exactly one schema contains all four:

| Field | Present in BiospecimenTemplate.properties |
|---|---|
| individualID | ✓ |
| specimenID | ✓ |
| aliquotID | ✓ |
| bodySite | ✓ |

### Caveats (under-claiming)
- The schema defines 12 properties total; the sample only populates 4. The other 8 properties (`experimentalCondition`, `modelAge`, `modelAgeUnit`, `modelSex`, `modelSpecies`, `modelSystemName`, `parentSpecimenID`, `tumorType`) are absent from the sample but may be required — I did not verify the `required` list against the sample, so this is a structural/field-name match, not a full validation.
- The HTAN schema collection has a `HTAN.BiospecimenData-v1.1.0-schema.json`, but it does not contain all four field names (it lacks one or more), so it was excluded by the grep filter.
- All four sample values are the literal placeholder `"sample string"`, so no value-level validation against enums/patterns was attempted.
grader_notes: Correctly committed to BiospecimenTemplate, though it cited the presence of all four fields rather than explicitly naming bodySite as the assay-vs-biospecimen disambiguator.

per_turn_tools.name: run_bash
per_turn_tools.input_preview: ls ncihtan_htan2-data-model/ | head -30 && echo "---" && ls nf-osi_nf-metadata-dictionary/ | head -30

per_turn_tools.name: run_bash
per_turn_tools.input_preview:
for f in $(grep -rl "aliquotID" .); do
  if grep -q "individualID" "$f" && grep -q "specimenID" "$f" && grep -q "bodySite" "$f"; then
    echo "MATCH: $f"
  fi
done

per_turn_tools.name: run_bash
per_turn_tools.input_preview:
python -c "
import json
d = json.load(open('./nf-osi_nf-metadata-dictionary/BiospecimenTemplate.json'))
print('Top-level keys:', list(d.keys())[:20])
print('Title/id:', d.get('\$id'), d.get('title'),
